rationale: Title Y is closer to Europace survey title conventions: concise, neutral, formal, and readable. Title X conveys more findings and sample detail, but it is too long, sounds prescriptive, and uses awkward phrases such as "drug-switching" and "last resort."
loser_weakness: Title X loses mainly on tone and clarity. It reads like a recommendation rather than a neutral survey title, and the wording is cumbersome despite higher informativeness.
